title: How The IDACORP (IDA) Story Is Evolving With Data Centers And Mixed Analyst Targets
content:
Get insights on thousands of stocks from the global community of over 7 million individual investors at Simply Wall St.

IDACORP’s modelled fair value has been updated to US$150.56 from US$147.00, a modest adjustment that keeps the focus on where the stock might sit relative to recent analyst price targets in the US$140s and US$150s. That shift comes as research firms weigh upbeat views on rate base growth and data center demand against concerns about valuation and sector appetite. Read on to see how to interpret these signals and track the evolving story around IDACORP’s potential.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value IDACORP.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Barclays and Mizuho highlight what they describe as strong fundamentals, with Barclays pointing to "industry-leading" rate base growth and Mizuho citing a strong 2025 report as key parts of their positive stance. Barclays lifted its target to US$149, while Mizuho moved to US$148, and Morgan Stanley set a target of US$155, indicating that several firms see room for the shares to align with higher valuation ranges. Barclays explicitly characterizes IDACORP’s valuation as attractive at recent share levels, framing upside potential in the context of its rate base profile. Morgan Stanley references data center pipelines as an area of interest around upcoming discussions, which some investors may view as an incremental growth angle to watch.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Wells Fargo downgraded IDACORP to Underweight from Equal Weight on valuation, indicating concern that the share price may already reflect a full outlook relative to its framework. UBS raised its target to US$142 but kept a Neutral rating, suggesting a more balanced view where upside and downside risks around execution, growth, and pricing look more evenly matched.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!NYSE:IDA 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 3 risks for IDACORP. See which could impact your investment.

How This Changes the Fair Value For IDACORP

Fair value updated to US$150.56 from US$147.00 in the modelled estimate. Revenue growth assumption kept at about 9.72%. Profit margin held at roughly 18.56%, with only a negligible adjustment. Future P/E revised to 23.64x from 23.08x. Discount rate held essentially steady at 6.98%.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives connect IDACORP's business story to a structured forecast and fair value so you can see how key assumptions fit together. They automatically refresh when analysts update expectations, new projects are announced, or risks are reassessed.

Story Continues

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on IDACORP to stay up to date on:

How regional population growth, electrification, and large projects like Micron fabs and data centers feed into long term electricity demand and recurring revenue. The scale and timing of IDACORP's planned transmission, storage, and generation investments, and how regulators treat cost recovery and allowed returns. Key risks around weather sensitive hydro output, regulatory outcomes, rising infrastructure and wildfire mitigation costs, and potential project delays or cancellations.

title: Here Is Why You Should Add Entergy to Your Portfolio Right Now
content:
Entergy Corporation ETR benefits from strategic capital investment in infrastructure development to expand its renewable assets and upgrade existing nuclear plants. Systematic investments in infrastructure enhance capacity, improve efficiency, ensure cleaner energy production and boost long-term growth.

Let’s focus on the factors that make this Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) stock a strong investment pick at the moment. You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here.

ETR’s Growth Projection & Surprise History

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for ETR’s 2026 and 2027 earnings has moved up 0.23% and 0.41%, respectively, in the past 60 days. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for ETR’s  2026 and 2027 sales is pinned at $13.78 billion and $15 billion, indicating year-over-year growth of 6.4% and 8.91%, respectively.

ETR’s long-term (three to five years) earnings growth rate is 11.50%.

ETR has surpassed earnings estimates in three of the trailing four quarters and reported on-par earnings in one quarter, resulting in an average positive earnings surprise 13.11%.

ETR’s Stable Investments

Entergy aims to strategically invest in grid modernization, decarbonize, diversify its portfolio and potentially construct additional generation assets. ETR plans to invest $43 billion during the 2026-2029-time frame. This four-year capital plan includes $17 billion for grid modernization, renewables expansion, customer growth, and $15 billion for new generation projects to diversify Entergy’s portfolio.

ETR’s Dividend History

Entergy has a dividend yield of 2.25% versus the Zacks S&P 500 composite’s average of 1.47%. The company’s current quarterly dividend rate is 64 cents, resulting in an annualized dividend of $2.56 per share.

ETR’s Return on Equity

Return on Equity (“ROE”) measures how efficiently a company is utilizing shareholders’ funds to generate returns, reflecting management efficiency and overall financial performance. At present, ETR’s ROE is 10.89%, higher than the industry average of 10.82%, indicating that the company is utilizing its funds more effectively than its industry peers.

ETR’s Solvency

ETR’s time earned ratio (TIE) at the end of the fourth quarter of 2025 was 2.6. The TIE ratio measures a company’s ability to meet long-term debt obligations, indicating how effectively operating earnings cover interest expenses and reflecting its overall financial stability and solvency.

Price Performance of ETR

In the past three months, Entergy shares have rallied 23.6% compared with the industry’s 10.3% growth.

Story Continues

Zacks Investment Research

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Other Stocks to Consider

Some other top-ranked stocks from the same industry are CenterPoint Energy CNP, IDACORP IDA and NiSource Inc. NI, each carrying a Zacks Rank #2. 

CNP, IDA and NI’s dividend yields are 2.13%, 2.45% and 2.56%, respectively.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for CenterPoint Energy, IDACORP and NiSource 2026 EPS is pegged at $1.91, $6.42 and $2.05, suggesting year-over-year growth of 8.52%, 8.81% and 7.89%, respectively.
